project.name: nike-ecommerce
project.description: Nike E-commerce is a full-stack web application for a Nike-style online store, built with Spring Boot, MySQL, JPA, JSP, and Spring Security. It includes a customer storefront, cart and checkout flows, and an admin back office for catalog and inventory management. ## Project Description

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project guidance, design notes, and supporting material that explain how the Nike commerce system is intended to be used and evolved.

layers.name: Build And Runtime Configuration
layers.description: Build, packaging, container, and environment configuration that shape how the application is compiled and deployed.

layers.name: Backend Platform
layers.description: Application entry points, shared Spring configuration, cross-cutting utilities, and infrastructure adapters reused across feature modules.

layers.name: Backend Commerce Features
layers.description: Feature-specific Java code implementing catalog, checkout, payment, identity, order, admin, and storefront business behavior.

layers.name: Server Rendered Views
layers.description: JSP templates, layouts, and view fragments that render the customer and administrator interfaces.

layers.name: Static Frontend Assets
layers.description: Client-side JavaScript, CSS, images, and related static resources that enhance the server-rendered UI.

layers.name: Tests And Data Assets
layers.description: Automated tests and structured data artifacts used to validate or exercise important parts of the application.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the main project guide and build definition to understand the application scope, stack, and operational expectations.

tour.title: Application Bootstrap
tour.description: Read the Spring Boot entry point and shared security setup to see how the server starts and how request access is controlled.

tour.title: Commerce Backend Features
tour.description: Follow the checkout and payment feature controllers and services to see how business flows are organized by feature package.

tour.title: Server Rendered Interface
tour.description: Inspect the customer checkout and product-facing JSP templates to see how the backend renders the storefront experience.

tour.title: Client Side Behavior
tour.description: Move to the static page scripts and styles that add dynamic behavior on top of the JSP-rendered pages.

tour.title: Deployment Surface
tour.description: Review the runtime property files and container definitions that package the application for local and server environments.

tour.title: Regression Safety
tour.description: Finish with the focused test suite that covers key backend seams such as checkout-adjacent services, payment, and exception handling.
